entry.title: PTA-Root-of-AVL-Tree
entry.content:
ķóśńø«µÅÅĶ┐░

An AVL tree is a self-balancing binary search tree. In an AVL tree, the heights of the two child subtrees of any node differ by at most one; if at any time they differ by more than one, rebalancing is done to restore this property. Figures 1-4 illustrate the rotation rules.

Now given a sequence of insertions, you are supposed to tell the root of the resulting AVL tree.
Input Specification:
Each input file contains one test case. For each case, the first line contains a positive integer N (Ōēż20) which is the total number of keys to be inserted. Then N distinct integer keys are given in the next line. All the numbers in a line are separated by a space.

Output Specification:
For each test case, print the root of the resulting AVL tree in one line.

Sample Input 1:
5
88 70 61 96 120
Sample Output 1:
70
Sample Input 2:
7
88 70 61 96 120 90 65
Sample Output 2:
88

Õłåµ×É

  • µ×äķĆĀÕ╣│ĶĪĪõ║īÕÅēµĀæÕŹ│ÕÅ»

õ╗ŻńĀü

ÕŠłÕżÜÕ£░µ¢╣ńø┤µÄźcopyõ║åµĄÖÕż¦µĢ░µŹ«ń╗ōµ×ämoocńÜäõ╗ŻńĀü-_-
